content.problemStatement:
You may have helped Chef and prevented Doof from destroying the even numbers. But, it has only angered Dr Doof even further. However, for his next plan, he needs some time. Therefore, Doof has built NNN walls to prevent Chef from interrupting him. You have to help Chef by telling him the number of walls he needs to destroy in order to reach Dr Doof.

Formally, the whole area can be represented as the first quadrant with the origin at the bottom-left corner. Dr. Doof is located at the origin (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0). There are NNN walls, the i-th wall is a straight line segment joining the points (ai,0)(a_i, 0)(ai​,0) and (0,ai)(0, a_i)(0,ai​). For every initial position of Chef (xj,yj)(x_j, y_j)(xj​,yj​), find the number of walls he needs to break before reaching Doof. Obviously, chef can't start from a point on the wall. Therefore, if (xj,yj)(x_j, y_j)(xj​,yj​) lies on any of the given walls, print −1-1−1 in a new line.

###Input


First line contains TTT, denoting the number of testcases.
The first line of every test case contains a single integer NNN denoting the number of walls Dr Doof has built.
The next line contains NNN space separated distinct integers each denoting aia_iai​.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ denoting the number of times Chef asks for your help.
The next QQQ lines contains two space separated integers xjx_jxj​ and yjy_jyj​, each denoting the co-ordinates of the starting point of Chef.
###Output

For each query, print the number of walls Chef needs to break in order to reach Dr Doof in a separate line. If Chef tries to start from a point on any of the walls, print −1-1−1.

###Constraints


1≤T≤2∗1021 \leq T \leq 2 * 10^21≤T≤2∗102
1≤N,Q≤2∗1051 \leq N, Q \leq 2 * 10^51≤N,Q≤2∗105
1≤ai≤1091 \leq a_i \leq 10^91≤ai​≤109
0≤xj,yj≤1090 \leq x_j, y_j \leq 10^90≤xj​,yj​≤109
a1<a2<a3<....<aNa_1 < a_2 < a_3 < .... < a_Na1​<a2​<a3​<....<aN​
Sum of NNN and QQQ over all testcases for a particular test file does not exceed 2∗1052 * 10^52∗105
constraints: 1 ≤ N, Q ≤ 2·10^5 (sum of N and Q over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains integer N, the number of walls. Second line contains N distinct integers a1<a2<...<aN. Third line contains integer Q, the number of queries. Each of the next Q lines contains two integers x and y, the starting coordinates of Chef.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a single line containing the number of walls Chef must break to reach the origin. If the starting point lies on any wall, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Walls at x+y=1 and x+y=3. For each query compute s=x+y and count walls with a_i < s, unless s equals a wall.

solution.approach: Each wall i is the line segment joining (ai,0) and (0,ai), i.e. the line x + y = ai inside the first quadrant. A point (x,y) lies on wall i iff x + y = ai. The origin is inside every wall. Therefore Chef must cross exactly those walls whose ai are strictly smaller than s = x + y. The answer is the count of ai < s, unless s equals some ai, in which case the answer is -1. Because the ai are given sorted, we can answer each query with a binary search (lower_bound). Complexity per query O(log N).
solution.timeComplexity: O((N + Q) log N) per test case (binary search for each query).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the array of wall positions.
